behavior_summary: This script defines a single U8P_KINGBDNS::look-type process (U8P_KINGBDNS::look) that runs when the player examines/looks at the NPC/object.
When triggered the process emits a single short bark with the text "bed " and then immediately suspends/ends the U8P_KINGBDNS::look process.
There are no menus, conditional branches, global flags read or written, or external calls — the behavior is a minimal, purely descriptive examine response.
notable_control_flow: U8P_KINGBDNS::look: entry on U8P_KINGBDNS::look/examine (processType 0x0208) -> Bark('bed ') -> Suspend/terminate the process.
No branching or looping; control flow is linear and ends after the single bark and suspend.
open_questions: What is the in-game meaning/context of the bark text "bed " (note trailing space) — is this a fragment, localization key, or full intended string?
The disassembly shows two low-confidence Unknown nodes ('{' and 'suspend') — do these represent any additional behavior or are they decompiler remnants?
Is this NPC used anywhere else (e.g., dialogue, triggers) beyond this simple U8P_KINGBDNS::look handler? The script contains no calls or flags to indicate other interactions.

main_function.name: U8P_KINGBDNS::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short examine/description response when the player looks at this NPC — plays a one-line bark ('bed ') and suspends the process.
entry_conditions: Triggered when the player uses the 'U8P_KINGBDNS::look' or 'examine' action on this NPC (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_KINGBDNS::look
function_map.role: examine/U8P_KINGBDNS::look handler that plays a single bark and suspends
